reasoning: XLK (tech) selling hard at -0.88% while XLF (financials) holds at -0.28%. This is a deterioration pattern with tech leading downside, not a full risk-off rotation. XLK underperforming SPY (-0.88% vs -0.41%) signals narrow tech-heavy weakness rather than broad selling. XLF's relative resilience prevents a high-confidence bearish signal, but tech weakness is a meaningful SPY headwind. Medium confidence bearish bias — sector internals show stress in the largest SPY component (tech) without compensating strength in financials.
key_observations: XLK: -0.88% down — tech is the primary weakness, largest SPY weighting under pressure
XLF: -0.28% — financials holding relatively stable, no panic signal
XLE: -0.35% — energy also weak, no defensive rotation story
Tech underperforming SPY: narrow leadership on downside, not broad deterioration
